howItWorks.intro: This scholarship calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Cost = (Tuition + Room & Board) – (Scholarships + Federal Aid)
Total Cost = Annual Net Cost × Years
Average aid covers 30-60% of total cost
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the average scholarship amount?
faq.answer: The average private scholarship is $5,000-10,000 per year. Institutional merit scholarships average $7,000-15,000. Full-ride scholarships cover everything but are rare — less than 0.1% of students receive one.

faq.question: Do scholarships affect financial aid?
faq.answer: Outside scholarships may reduce need-based aid. Many schools reduce loans first (good), but some reduce grants (bad). Always ask the financial aid office about their scholarship displacement policy.

faq.question: What is the average net price of college?
faq.answer: After all aid, the average net price is $15,000-20,000/year at public universities and $25,000-35,000 at private colleges. This is significantly less than the sticker price that schools advertise.

howWeCalculate.intro: This scholarship calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This scholarship calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable estimates.
